1.24: Now to the one who has the power to keep you from falling and to bring you into his glorious presence—spotless and filled with joy—
1.12: These people are dangerous blemishes at your fellowship meals—eating alongside you without a care, taking care only of themselves. They are clouds that blow by without giving any rain; autumn trees stripped of fruit and pulled up by the roots—dead twice over.
1.2: May mercy, peace, and love be lavished on you.
1.21: Keep yourselves in God's love while you wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ—the mercy that leads to eternal life.
1.3: Dear friends, I was fully intending to write to you about the salvation we share together—but I felt I had to write urgently instead, to urge you to fight hard for the faith that was entrusted, once and for all, to God's people.
1.17: But you, dear friends, remember what the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ said would happen.
1.20: But you, dear friends, must build yourselves up in your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Spirit.
1.6: And the angels who abandoned their own positions of authority and left their proper home—God has kept them in eternal chains in deep darkness, waiting for the judgment of the great day.
1.1: I, Jude, am a servant of Jesus Christ and a brother of James. I am writing to all who have been called by God the Father—loved by him and kept safe for Jesus Christ.
1.14: Enoch, the seventh generation from Adam, prophesied about these people: 'Look! The Lord is coming with tens of thousands of his holy ones—
1.13: They are wild ocean waves that churn up only their own shame. They are wandering stars, destined for the blackest darkness forever.
1.8: These people are the same—they are dreamers who pollute their own bodies, despise authority, and mock the glorious ones.
1.5: Even though you already know all this, I want to remind you: the Lord rescued his people out of Egypt, but later destroyed those who refused to believe.
1.7: Similarly, Sodom and Gomorrah and the surrounding towns gave themselves over to sexual immorality and unnatural desire. They stand as a permanent warning—suffering the punishment of eternal fire.
1.9: But even Michael the archangel, when he was arguing with the devil over the body of Moses, didn't dare to slander him. He simply said, 'The Lord rebuke you!'
1.10: But these people mock anything they don't understand, and the things they do understand—by animal instinct—are the very things that destroy them.
1.15: to carry out judgment on everyone—to convict every person of every ungodly act they have committed in their ungodliness, and for every defiant word that ungodly sinners have spoken against him.'
1.25: to the only God our Savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord—be glory, majesty, power, and authority, before all time, now and forever. Amen.
1.4: This is urgent because certain people have secretly slipped into your community—people whose judgment was written down long ago. These ungodly individuals twist God's grace into a license for sin and deny Jesus Christ, our only Master and Lord.
1.11: What terrible things await them! They have walked the path of Cain, rushed headlong into Balaam's error for financial gain, and been destroyed by Korah's kind of rebellion.
1.23: Pull others out of the fire and save them. And for others, show mercy mixed with caution—hating even the clothing contaminated by their sinful living.
1.19: These are the people who create divisions—driven by natural instincts with no share in the Spirit.
1.18: They warned you: 'In the last days, mockers will arise who follow their own ungodly desires.'
1.16: These people are constant complainers who do whatever they feel like doing. They boast loudly about themselves and flatter others whenever it benefits them.
1.22: Show mercy to those who are wavering in their faith.
